name: IPR-1A "Mold"
description: The IPR-1A Mold is the first-generation industrial surface printer, designed for fabricating heavy equipment from locally-sourced regolith binders and pre-positioned metal feedstock. Its primary process chain is sand casting — pouring regolith-metal slurries into wire-printed ceramic molds — followed by resistive sintering to cure the final part. Though slow by later standards, the Mold can produce large structural frames for refineries and mining equipment with minimal off-world feedstock. Rated for industrial-category output only: refineries, miners, prospectors, and printers.
